{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578","id":2663993903,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6eyVIv","number":56578,"title":"replace should not be defined for non-iterable arguments","user":{"login":"tillfalko","id":62334868,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYyMzM0ODY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/62334868?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko","html_url":"https://github.com/tillfalko","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-16T07:59:14Z","updated_at":"2024-11-16T07:59:14Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"From the documentation:

>     replace(A, old_new::Pair...; [count::Integer])
> 
> Return a copy of collection `A` where, for each pair `old=>new` in `old_new`,
> all occurrences of `old` are replaced by `new`.

Yet `A` is not actually constrained to collections. This seems like a wasted opportunity to use Julia's type system to prevent bugs like the following.
```julia
x = 2.2
replace(x, NaN=>0)
```
`ERROR: MethodError: no method matching similar(::Float64, ::Type{Float64})`

### Possible Solutions (Discussion)
1. Somehow restrict the methods of `replace` to collections. I'm not sure if this is possible as there does not seem to be an `AbstractCollection` that encompasses `AbstractArray`, `AbstractSet`, `AbstractString`, etc.
2. Support non-collection values of `A`. While the example above could be done with `ifelse` or the ternary operator, replace could be very useful for non-iterable when using pipe-syntax.
3. {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578","id":2663993903,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6eyVIv","number":56578,"title":"replace should not be defined for non-iterable arguments","user":{"login":"tillfalko","id":62334868,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYyMzM0ODY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/62334868?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko","html_url":"https://github.com/tillfalko","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-11-16T07:59:14Z","updated_at":"2024-11-17T08:34:23Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"From the documentation:

>     replace(A, old_new::Pair...; [count::Integer])
> 
> Return a copy of collection `A` where, for each pair `old=>new` in `old_new`,
> all occurrences of `old` are replaced by `new`.

Yet `A` is not actually constrained to collections. This seems like a wasted opportunity to use Julia's type system to prevent bugs like the following.
```julia
x = 2.2
replace(x, NaN=>0)
```
`ERROR: MethodError: no method matching similar(::Float64, ::Type{Float64})`

### Possible Solutions (Discussion)
1. Somehow restrict the methods of `replace` to collections. I'm not sure if this is possible as there does not seem to be an `AbstractCollection` that encompasses `AbstractArray`, `AbstractSet`, `AbstractString`, etc.
2. Support non-collection values of `A`. While the example above could be done with `ifelse` or the ternary operator, replace could be very useful for non-iterable values of `A`. For example I often find myself wanting to write
```julia
    (some function)
    return replace(x, Inf=>NaN, -Inf=>NaN)
end
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2481037939","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578#issuecomment-2481037939","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56578","id":2481037939,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6T4aJz","user":{"login":"tillfalko","id":62334868,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYyMzM0ODY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/62334868?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko","html_url":"https://github.com/tillfalko","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tillfalko/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-17T08:34:22Z","updated_at":"2024-11-17T08:34:22Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"`sort` and `in` are both 'meant' to be used on collections. Let's see how they behave when called with e.g. floats.

When you call `sort(3.3)` you get `ERROR: MethodError: no method matching sort(::Float64)`, which makes sense.

When you call `3.3 in 3.3` you get `true`, which is less intuitive but arguably useful.

I would argue that calling `replace(3.3, NaN=>0)` should return either `3.3` or `ERROR: MethodError: no method matching replace(::Float64, ::Pair{Float64, Int64})`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2481037939/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Yet `A` is not actually constrained to collections. This seems like a wasted opportunity to use Julia's type system to prevent bugs like the following.
```julia

`ERROR: MethodError: no method matching similar(::Float64, ::Type{Float64})`

    return replace(x, Inf=>NaN, -Inf=>NaN)

I see. In that case would it not make sense for `replace` to support `Number` the way it does `Tuple`?

Yet `A` is not actually constrained to collections. This seems like a wasted opportunity to use Julia's type system to prevent bugs like the following.

`ERROR: MethodError: no method matching similar(::Float64, ::Type{Float64})`

    (some function)
